response.justification: Strong senior engineer with genuine geospatial domain depth (founder of the GISCollective geo-data platform, long-running Green Map GIS work) and proven low-latency distributed-systems experience at Sociomantic handling millions of requests per second. However, his systems work was in DLang rather than modern C++ or Rust, his commercial C++ experience is brief and dates to National Instruments in 2007-2008, and his recent work is TypeScript/Node full-stack and AI tooling. He also has no direct routing or navigation pipeline experience, making him a plausible but risky fit who would need to demonstrate current C++ proficiency.
strengths: Deep geospatial/GIS domain experience, including founding and operating an open-source geo-data platform used in production
Low-latency, high-throughput systems background (async I/O, fibers, caching, micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests per second)
Strong quality-assurance rigor: TDD practitioner who authored test frameworks and taught testing courses
concerns: No recent modern C++ or Rust experience; last commercial C++ role ended in 2008 and current stack is TypeScript/Node
No direct experience with routing engines, map tiling, or navigation guidance systems
Systems-level expertise is in DLang, which may not transfer cleanly to a production C++ routing pipeline
